name: dependency-management-deps-audit
description: You are a dependency security expert specializing in vulnerability scanning, license compliance, and supply chain security. Analyze project dependencies for known vulnerabilities, licensing issues, outdated packages, and provide actionable remediation strategies.
capabilities: Scan dependencies across multiple package managers (npm, pip, gem, maven, go, cargo, composer)
Check dependencies against vulnerability databases (CVE, NVD, npm advisory)
Analyze license compatibility and identify compliance issues
Identify outdated packages and prioritize updates by security risk
Detect supply chain risks including typosquatting and maintainer changes
Generate automated remediation scripts and pull request templates
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The detected patterns exist in markdown documentation files containing code examples, not executable code. The implementation-playbook.md (767 lines) and SKILL.md (47 lines) are instructional documents showing users how to implement security scanning tools. No actual shell execution, network calls, or filesystem operations occur in the skill itself.
valueStatement: Managing dependencies securely is challenging with constant new vulnerabilities and complex license requirements. This skill automates vulnerability scanning, license compliance checks, and provides prioritized remediation strategies.
userTitle: Audit dependencies for vulnerabilities and license issues
limitations: Requires access to dependency manifest files (package.json, requirements.txt, etc.)
Cannot execute package managers or install tools without user environment access
Vulnerability database checks depend on external API availability
Does not automatically apply fixes without user confirmation

useCases.title: Pre-release security audit
useCases.description: Before shipping production code, scan all dependencies to identify and fix critical vulnerabilities. Generate compliance reports for security reviews.

useCases.targetUser: Open source maintainer ensuring license compliance
useCases.title: License compliance verification
useCases.description: Review all dependency licenses to ensure compatibility with your project license. Identify GPL or proprietary licenses that may create legal risks.

useCases.targetUser: Development team lead maintaining code health
useCases.title: Dependency maintenance workflow
useCases.description: Set up automated scanning to identify outdated packages. Receive prioritized lists of updates based on security fixes and age.

promptTemplates.title: Basic vulnerability scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan my project dependencies for known vulnerabilities. Identify the package name, current version, vulnerability severity, and recommended fix version.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick security check on a Node.js project

promptTemplates.title: License compliance report
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze all dependency licenses in this project. My project uses MIT license. Identify any incompatible licenses and explain the legal risks for each.
promptTemplates.scenario: Verifying license compliance before open sourcing

promptTemplates.title: Prioritized update plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my dependencies and create a prioritized update plan. Rank by security risk first, then by how outdated each package is. Include estimated effort for each update.

promptTemplates.title: Supply chain security audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a comprehensive supply chain security audit. Check for typosquatting risks, unusual maintainer changes, and packages with suspicious behavior patterns. Flag any packages that need manual review.
promptTemplates.scenario: Deep security review after supply chain attack concerns

bestPractices: Run vulnerability scans in CI/CD before merging pull requests
Keep a curated list of approved licenses for your organization
Pin exact dependency versions and update through automated PRs
antiPatterns: Ignoring critical vulnerabilities because tests pass locally
Using version ranges like '*' that allow unexpected updates
Adding dependencies without reviewing their license terms

faq.question: How does this skill detect vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: The skill checks your dependencies against public vulnerability databases including npm advisory, PyPI security, and OSS Index. It matches package names and versions to known CVEs.

faq.question: Can this skill automatically fix vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: The skill generates remediation scripts and update commands, but requires your confirmation before making changes. It can create pull request templates for review.

faq.question: What package managers are supported?
faq.answer: Supports npm/yarn (JavaScript), pip/poetry (Python), gem (Ruby), maven/gradle (Java), go mod, cargo (Rust), composer (PHP), and nuget (.NET).

faq.question: How often should I run dependency audits?
faq.answer: Run scans on every pull request for critical projects. For maintenance, weekly or monthly scans catch new vulnerabilities. Set up alerts for critical CVEs affecting your dependencies.

faq.question: What should I do about GPL dependencies in my MIT project?
faq.answer: GPL licenses are incompatible with MIT distribution. Options include: replacing with MIT/Apache alternatives, isolating GPL code in separate processes, or changing your project license to GPL.

faq.question: How do I verify a suspicious package name?
faq.answer: Check the package repository, review download counts, verify maintainer identity, and compare against known legitimate packages. Typosquats often have low downloads and recent creation dates.
